name: Doris's Pralines
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix all ingredients except butter, vanilla, and pecans.
2. Cook on low heat until the mixture forms a soft ball when dropped in cold water. Remove from heat.
3. Add butter, vanilla, and pecans; beat until the mixture holds its shape.
4. Spoon onto buttered wax paper (place old newspaper under the wax paper).
5. If the candy hardens before all is spooned out, add a little water and continue heating gently. Or let it stand on low heat while spooning out.
6. Enjoy freshly made pralines made from fresh Louisiana pecans – they truly enhance life.
(Note: The original text included additional personal anecdotes about Justin Wilson's experiences with Doris. These have been omitted to maintain focus solely on the recipe.)
